```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(DEoptim)
library(lmomco)
library(DEoptim)
```


```{r}

# =====================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION SCRIPT
# =====================================================================

# 1. Setup Paths (Your existing logic)
gdrive_path <- paste(LETTERS[file.exists(paste0(LETTERS, ":/My Drive"))], ":/My Drive", sep = "")
project_path <- file.path(gdrive_path, "Academic_git/DownScaling")
generalData_path <- file.path(gdrive_path, "General_Data")

# 2. Define the source data directory (e.g., for Germany)
data_dir <- file.path(generalData_path, "GSDR/QC_d data - Germany")

# 3. DYNAMICALLY CREATE EXPORT DIRECTORY NAME
# basename(data_dir) extracts "QC_d data - Germany"
folder_name <- basename(data_dir)
folder_name <- sub(".*- ", "", folder_name)

export_folder_name <- paste("Cross_Corr_Downs", folder_name)

# 4. Define the full individual_dir path
individual_dir <- file.path(project_path, export_folder_name)

# 5. Create the directory on your drive if it doesn't exist yet
if (!dir.exists(individual_dir)) {
  dir.create(individual_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  message("Created new directory: ", individual_dir)
}

# 6. Locate Station Files
station_files <- list.files(path = data_dir, pattern = "\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)
```



```{r}


station_files <- station_files[1:2]

# --- 1. SETUP STORAGE ---
raw_data_list <- list()
all_inspection_lists <- list()
all_clean_bins_lists <- list()

# --- 2. PASS ONE: READ DATA & EXTRACT DATES ---
for (current_station_file in station_files) {
  
  station_name <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(current_station_file))
  cat("Reading station:", station_name, "\n")
  
  # Read Data
  df <- read.table(current_station_file, skip = 21, col.names = "precip")
  df$precip[df$precip == -999] <- NA
  
  # Read Dates
  header_lines <- readLines(current_station_file, n = 21)
  start_date_str <- sub("Start datetime: ", "", grep("^Start datetime:", header_lines, value = TRUE))
  end_date_str <- sub("End datetime: ", "", grep("^End datetime:", header_lines, value = TRUE))
  
  start_ts <- ymd_h(start_date_str)
  end_ts <- ymd_h(end_date_str)
  df$date <- seq(start_ts, end_ts, by = "hour")
  
  # Store the raw dataframe in our list, named by the station
  raw_data_list[[station_name]] <- df
}

# --- 3. FIND COMMON DATE WINDOW ---
# Find the latest start date and the earliest end date among all stations
common_start <- max(do.call(c, lapply(raw_data_list, function(df) min(df$date))))
common_end <- min(do.call(c, lapply(raw_data_list, function(df) max(df$date))))

cat("\n======================================================\n")
cat("Common Time Window:\n")
cat("Start:", as.character(common_start), "\n")
cat("End:  ", as.character(common_end), "\n")
cat("======================================================\n")

# --- 4. PASS TWO: FILTER AND AGGREGATE ---
Base_time_chunks_per_hour <- 4
empirical_k_training <- seq(1:10) * 24 * Base_time_chunks_per_hour
empirical_k_validation <- c(1,2,4,6,8,10,12) * Base_time_chunks_per_hour
all_k <- c(empirical_k_validation, empirical_k_training)

for (station_name in names(raw_data_list)) {
  
  cat("Processing and aggregating:", station_name, "\n")
  
  # Pull the raw data for this station and filter to common dates
  df <- raw_data_list[[station_name]]
  df <- df %>% filter(date >= common_start & date <= common_end)
  
  # Convert to rate
  df$precip_rate <- df$precip / Base_time_chunks_per_hour
  df$hour_index <- 0:(nrow(df) - 1)
  
  # Setup temporary lists for this specific station
  station_inspection_list <- list()
  station_clean_bins_list <- list()
  
  # Aggregation loop
  for (i in seq_along(all_k)) {
    agg_length <- all_k[i] / Base_time_chunks_per_hour
    
    df_detailed <- df %>%
      mutate(bin_id = hour_index %/% agg_length) %>%
      group_by(bin_id) %>%
      mutate(
        hours_in_bin = n(),
        bin_mean_precip = mean(precip_rate, na.rm = FALSE),
        is_valid_bin = (!is.na(bin_mean_precip) & hours_in_bin == agg_length)
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    list_name <- paste0("scale_", agg_length, "Hours")
    station_inspection_list[[list_name]] <- df_detailed
  }
  
  # Clean Bins loop
  for (scale_name in names(station_inspection_list)) {
    df_detailed <- station_inspection_list[[scale_name]]
    valid_bins <- df_detailed %>%
      filter(is_valid_bin) %>%
      group_by(bin_id) %>%
      summarise(
        agg_date = min(date),
        bin_mean_precip = first(bin_mean_precip),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    station_clean_bins_list[[scale_name]] <- valid_bins
  }
  
  # Save the station's finished lists into the global parent lists
  all_inspection_lists[[station_name]] <- station_inspection_list
  all_clean_bins_lists[[station_name]] <- station_clean_bins_list
}


```




```{r}
# Extract the names of your two stations
station_names <- names(all_clean_bins_lists)
stat1 <- station_names[1]
stat2 <- station_names[2]

cat("\n======================================================\n")
cat("Calculating Cross-Correlation (Lag 0) between:\n")
cat("1.", stat1, "\n")
cat("2.", stat2, "\n")
cat("======================================================\n")

# Get all the scale names (e.g., "scale_1Hours", "scale_24Hours")
scale_names <- names(all_clean_bins_lists[[stat1]])

# Create an empty dataframe to store the results
correlation_results <- data.frame(
  Scale = character(),
  Numeric_Hours = numeric(),
  Valid_Bin_Pairs = integer(),
  Correlation_Lag_0 = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Loop through each aggregation scale
for (scale_name in scale_names) {
  
  # Extract the clean dataframes for both stations at this specific scale
  df1 <- all_clean_bins_lists[[stat1]][[scale_name]]
  df2 <- all_clean_bins_lists[[stat2]][[scale_name]]
  
  # Inner join by bin_id ensures we only compare pairs where BOTH stations have valid data
  joined_df <- inner_join(df1, df2, by = "bin_id", suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
  
  n_pairs <- nrow(joined_df)
  
  # Calculate correlation (requires at least 2 data points)
  if (n_pairs > 1) {
    corr_val <- cor(joined_df$bin_mean_precip_1, joined_df$bin_mean_precip_2, method = "pearson")
  } else {
    corr_val <- NA # Not enough data to correlate at this scale
  }
  
  # Extract the number from "scale_XHours" so we can sort the final table logically
  num_hours <- as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", scale_name))
  
  # Append the row to our results dataframe
  correlation_results <- rbind(correlation_results, data.frame(
    Scale = scale_name,
    Numeric_Hours = num_hours,
    Valid_Bin_Pairs = n_pairs,
    Correlation_Lag_0 = corr_val
  ))
}

# Sort the results from the smallest time scale to the largest
correlation_results <- correlation_results %>% 
  arrange(Numeric_Hours) %>%
  select(-Numeric_Hours) # Remove the sorting column to keep the output clean

# View the final table
print(correlation_results)
# Extract numbers from "scale_1Hours"
correlation_results$Scale_num <- as.numeric(
  gsub("scale_|Hours", "", correlation_results$Scale)
)


```



```{r}
# =====================================================================
# CROSS-CORRELATION FUNCTIONS (Monotonically Increasing)
# =====================================================================

# -- 2-Parameter Functions --
# Equation (10): Weibull-H>
H_W_2p_cross <- function(k, H_inf, k_star, q_k_star, a) {
  exponent <- (k / k_star)^a
  base_fraction <- 1 - (q_k_star / H_inf)
  
  # Ensure base is not negative to avoid NaN issues in R
  base_fraction <- ifelse(base_fraction <= 0, 1e-6, base_fraction) 
  
  H_inf * (1 - (base_fraction ^ exponent))
}

# Equation (11): Lomax-H>
H_L_2p_cross <- function(k, H_inf, k_star, q_k_star, b) {
  ln_numerator <- log(1 + b * k)
  ln_denominator <- log(1 + b * k_star)
  exponent <- ln_numerator / ln_denominator
  base_fraction <- 1 - (q_k_star / H_inf)
  
  base_fraction <- ifelse(base_fraction <= 0, 1e-6, base_fraction)
  
  H_inf * (1 - (base_fraction ^ exponent))
}

# -- 1-Parameter Functions (Assuming H_inf = 1) --
H_W_1p_cross <- function(k, k_star, q_k_star, a) {
  H_inf <- 1 
  exponent <- (k / k_star)^a
  base_fraction <- 1 - (q_k_star / H_inf)
  
  base_fraction <- ifelse(base_fraction <= 0, 1e-6, base_fraction)
  
  H_inf * (1 - (base_fraction ^ exponent))
}

H_L_1p_cross <- function(k, k_star, q_k_star, b) {
  H_inf <- 1 
  ln_numerator <- log(1 + b * k)
  ln_denominator <- log(1 + b * k_star)
  exponent <- ln_numerator / ln_denominator
  base_fraction <- 1 - (q_k_star / H_inf)
  
  base_fraction <- ifelse(base_fraction <= 0, 1e-6, base_fraction)
  
  H_inf * (1 - (base_fraction ^ exponent))
}

# -- MSE Functions for Optimization --
mse_W_2p_cross <- function(par, k, y, k_star, q_k_star) mean((y - H_W_2p_cross(k, par[1], k_star, q_k_star, par[2]))^2, na.rm=TRUE)
mse_L_2p_cross <- function(par, k, y, k_star, q_k_star) mean((y - H_L_2p_cross(k, par[1], k_star, q_k_star, par[2]))^2, na.rm=TRUE)

mse_W_1p_cross <- function(par, k, y, k_star, q_k_star) mean((y - H_W_1p_cross(k, k_star, q_k_star, par[1]))^2, na.rm=TRUE)
mse_L_1p_cross <- function(par, k, y, k_star, q_k_star) mean((y - H_L_1p_cross(k, k_star, q_k_star, par[1]))^2, na.rm=TRUE)
```



```{r}

# Assuming your raw dataframe is named 'correlation_results'
fit_data <- correlation_results %>%
  # Rename the columns to match what the optimization script expects
  rename(
    k_hours = Scale_num, 
    cross_corr = Correlation_Lag_0
  ) %>%
  # Keep only the columns necessary for fitting
  select(k_hours, cross_corr) %>%
  # Ensure it is strictly sorted from smallest to largest time scale
  arrange(k_hours)

# View the formatted data
head(fit_data)



# =====================================================================
# 1. SPLIT DATA: TRAINING (>= 24h) & VALIDATION (< 24h)
# =====================================================================
k_star <- 24

# Filter data into calibration and validation sets
training_data <- fit_data %>% filter(k_hours >= k_star) %>% arrange(k_hours)
validation_data <- fit_data %>% filter(k_hours < k_star & k_hours >= 1) %>% arrange(k_hours)

# Extract variables for DEoptim
k_obs <- training_data$k_hours
y_obs <- training_data$cross_corr
q_k_star <- training_data$cross_corr[training_data$k_hours == k_star][1]

# =====================================================================
# 2. FIT MODELS (Using ONLY Training Data)
# =====================================================================
de_ctrl <- DEoptim.control(
  trace = FALSE, 
  itermax = 5000,   # Increased from 500 
  NP = 500,          # Larger population for a more thorough search
  reltol = 1e-11,    # Tighter tolerance for better precision
  steptol = 500     # Number of steps to wait for improvement
 )

# Bounds for H_inf: Must be strictly > q_k_star and <= 1.0
lower_bounds_2p <- c( 1e-5, 1e-6) # [H_inf, shape_parameter]
upper_bounds_2p <- c(1.0, 1000)

# 2-Parameter Fits
fit_W_2p <- DEoptim(mse_W_2p_cross, lower = lower_bounds_2p, upper = upper_bounds_2p, 
                    k = k_obs, y = y_obs, k_star = k_star, q_k_star = q_k_star, control = de_ctrl)

fit_L_2p <- DEoptim(mse_L_2p_cross, lower = lower_bounds_2p, upper = upper_bounds_2p, 
                    k = k_obs, y = y_obs, k_star = k_star, q_k_star = q_k_star, control = de_ctrl)

# 1-Parameter Fits (H_inf fixed to 1 internally)
fit_W_1p <- DEoptim(mse_W_1p_cross, lower = 1e-6, upper = 1000, 
                    k = k_obs, y = y_obs, k_star = k_star, q_k_star = q_k_star, control = de_ctrl)

fit_L_1p <- DEoptim(mse_L_1p_cross, lower = 1e-6, upper = 1000, 
                    k = k_obs, y = y_obs, k_star = k_star, q_k_star = q_k_star, control = de_ctrl)

# Compile optimized parameters into a clean dataframe
optimized_params <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Weibull_2p", "PowerLaw_2p", "Weibull_1p", "PowerLaw_1p"),
  H_inf = c(fit_W_2p$optim$bestmem[1], fit_L_2p$optim$bestmem[1], 1, 1),
  Param_shape = c(fit_W_2p$optim$bestmem[2], fit_L_2p$optim$bestmem[2], 
                  fit_W_1p$optim$bestmem[1], fit_L_1p$optim$bestmem[1]),
  Training_MSE = c(fit_W_2p$optim$bestval, fit_L_2p$optim$bestval, 
                   fit_W_1p$optim$bestval, fit_L_1p$optim$bestval)
)

# =====================================================================
# 3. VALIDATE ON SUB-DAILY SCALES (1 to 23 hours)
# =====================================================================
k_val <- validation_data$k_hours
y_val_actual <- validation_data$cross_corr

# Calculate predictions for the validation data
pred_W_2p_val <- H_W_2p_cross(k_val, optimized_params$H_inf[1], k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[1])
pred_L_2p_val <- H_L_2p_cross(k_val, optimized_params$H_inf[2], k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[2])
pred_W_1p_val <- H_W_1p_cross(k_val, k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[3])
pred_L_1p_val <- H_L_1p_cross(k_val, k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[4])

# Calculate Validation MSE and add it to our parameters dataframe
optimized_params$Validation_MSE <- c(
  mean((y_val_actual - pred_W_2p_val)^2, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean((y_val_actual - pred_L_2p_val)^2, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean((y_val_actual - pred_W_1p_val)^2, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean((y_val_actual - pred_L_1p_val)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
)

print(optimized_params)

# =====================================================================
# 4. EXTRAPOLATE DOWN TO 15 MINS AND COMPILE FINAL PREDICTIONS
# =====================================================================
# Create a master sequence of k values: 0.25 (15 mins), validation scales, and training scales
k_all <- c(0.25, validation_data$k_hours, training_data$k_hours)

all_predictions <- data.frame(
  k_hours = k_all,
  # We use NA for 0.25 because we don't have actual empirical data for it
  Actual_Corr = c(NA, validation_data$cross_corr, training_data$cross_corr) 
)

# Generate predictions across all scales for all models
all_predictions$Weibull_2p <- H_W_2p_cross(k_all, optimized_params$H_inf[1], k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[1])
all_predictions$PowerLaw_2p <- H_L_2p_cross(k_all, optimized_params$H_inf[2], k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[2])
all_predictions$Weibull_1p <- H_W_1p_cross(k_all, k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[3])
all_predictions$PowerLaw_1p <- H_L_1p_cross(k_all, k_star, q_k_star, optimized_params$Param_shape[4])


```


```{r}
# =====================================================================
# 5. IDENTIFY THE BEST MODEL (Lowest Validation MSE)
# =====================================================================

# Isolate the row with the lowest Validation MSE
best_model_info <- optimized_params %>%
  slice_min(order_by = Validation_MSE, n = 1)

best_model_name <- best_model_info$Model
best_model_mse <- best_model_info$Validation_MSE

cat("\n======================================================\n")
cat("BEST MODEL SELECTION (Validation Data: 1h to 23h)\n")
cat("======================================================\n")
cat("Winning Model:    ", best_model_name, "\n")
cat("Validation MSE:   ", best_model_mse, "\n")

# Automatically extract the 15-minute (k = 0.25) prediction for the winning model
best_15min_prediction <- all_predictions[[best_model_name]][all_predictions$k_hours == 0.25]

cat("Extrapolated 15m: ", best_15min_prediction, "\n")
cat("======================================================\n")
```










```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# =====================================================================
# 1. PREPARE EMPIRICAL DATA (Points)
# =====================================================================
# Filter out anything below k=1 since the plot starts at 1
df_points <- fit_data %>%
  filter(k_hours >= 1) %>%
  rename(k = k_hours, rho = cross_corr) %>%
  mutate(Data_Type = ifelse(k >= 24, "Calib. data", "Valid. data")) %>%
  # Convert to factor to force the legend order exactly as shown in the image
  mutate(Data_Type = factor(Data_Type, levels = c("Calib. data", "Valid. data")))

# =====================================================================
# 2. GENERATE SMOOTH PREDICTION LINES (Curves)
# =====================================================================
k_star <- 24
q_k_star <- df_points$rho[df_points$k == k_star][1]

# Create a dense sequence of k values for perfectly smooth curves on a log scale
k_smooth <- exp(seq(log(1), log(250), length.out = 300))

# Helper function to extract parameters safely from optimized_params
get_param <- function(mod_name, param_col) {
  val <- optimized_params[[param_col]][optimized_params$Model == mod_name]
  return(val[1])
}

# Calculate predicted values using the exact functions we defined earlier
df_lines <- data.frame(k = k_smooth)
df_lines$`Wei-2p` <- H_W_2p_cross(k_smooth, get_param("Weibull_2p", "H_inf"), k_star, q_k_star, get_param("Weibull_2p", "Param_shape"))
df_lines$`Wei-1p` <- H_W_1p_cross(k_smooth, k_star, q_k_star, get_param("Weibull_1p", "Param_shape"))
df_lines$`Lom-2p` <- H_L_2p_cross(k_smooth, get_param("PowerLaw_2p", "H_inf"), k_star, q_k_star, get_param("PowerLaw_2p", "Param_shape"))
df_lines$`Lom-1p` <- H_L_1p_cross(k_smooth, k_star, q_k_star, get_param("PowerLaw_1p", "Param_shape"))

# Reshape lines to long format and set factor levels for legend ordering
df_lines_long <- df_lines %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -k, names_to = "Model", values_to = "Prediction") %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = c("Lom-1p", "Lom-2p", "Wei-1p", "Wei-2p")))

# =====================================================================
# 3. BUILD THE EXACT PLOT
# =====================================================================
ggplot() +
  # 1. Vertical dashed guidelines at k=1 and k=24
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, 24), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # 2. Add Model Lines (mapped to color and linetype)
  geom_line(data = df_lines_long, aes(x = k, y = Prediction, color = Model, linetype = Model), linewidth = 1) +
  
  # 3. Add Empirical Points (mapped to fill to keep legend separate from lines)
  geom_point(data = df_points, aes(x = k, y = rho, fill = Data_Type), 
             shape = 21, color = "transparent", size = 2.5) +
  
  # 4. X-Axis: Logarithmic scale with exact breaks from the image
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = "log", 
    breaks = c(1, 2, 4, 12, 24, 48, 96, 216),
    labels = c(1, 2, 4, 12, 24, 48, 96, 216),
    expand = c(0.05, 0.05)
  ) +
  
  # 5. Y-Axis: Linear scale, limits 0 to 1, with identical secondary axis
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1), 
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    sec.axis = dup_axis(name = expression(rho^"(i,j)"))
  ) +
  
  # 6. Exact Color and Linetype Mappings
  scale_color_manual(
    name = NULL,
    values = c("Lom-1p" = "red", "Lom-2p" = "red", "Wei-1p" = "blue", "Wei-2p" = "blue")
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    name = NULL,
    values = c("Lom-1p" = "solid", "Lom-2p" = "dashed", "Wei-1p" = "solid", "Wei-2p" = "dashed")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = NULL,
    values = c("Calib. data" = "black", "Valid. data" = "orange")
  ) +
  
  # 7. Labels
  labs(
    x = "Temporal scale, k [h]", 
    y = expression(rho^"(i,j)"), 
    title = "St. ID: Station 1 vs Station 2"
  ) +
  
  # 8. Theme customization to match the provided image
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    # Gridlines (dashed light gray)
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linetype = "dashed", color = "gray85"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # Title centering
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    
    # Legend Box styling (Bottom Right, inside plot)
    legend.position = c(0.9,0.25 ),
    legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "cm"), # Tightly pack the legend items
    legend.margin = margin(t = 2, r = 5, b = 2, l = 5)
  ) +
  
  # 9. Format the Legend Keys (combine them neatly)
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1, override.aes = list(shape = 21, size = 3, color = c("black", "orange"))),
    color = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(linewidth = 1)),
    linetype = guide_legend(order = 2)
  )
```
